description: CT abdomen with contrast - handwritten addendum
ground_truth:
CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST

Date: 15/01/2024
Patient: Michael Davis  DOB: 03/12/1967  MRN: 991245

Indication: Abdominal pain, evaluate for appendicitis or diverticulitis.

Technique:
Non-contrast images followed by portal venous phase images after IV contrast.
5mm axial reconstructions. Coronal and sagittal reformats.

Findings:

Liver: Normal size and attenuation. No focal lesion.
Gallbladder: Distended. Multiple small gallstones identified. No wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. No cholecystitis.

Pancreas: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic ductal dilation. No focal lesion.

Spleen: Normal.

Adrenal Glands: Normal bilaterally.

Kidneys: Bilateral kidneys enhance normally without hydronephrosis. No renal mass or calculi.

Abdominal Aorta: Normal caliber, no aneurysm.

Lymph Nodes: No pathologic retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

Bowel:
Small bowel: Normal caliber and wall enhancement.
Colon: Diverticulosis noted in the sigmoid colon without pericolonic inflammation or abscess.
Appendix: Normal caliber, visualized in its entirety. No periappendiceal inflammation.

Impression:
1. No evidence of appendicitis.
2. Diverticulosis of the sigmoid colon without diverticulitis.
3. Cholelithiasis without cholecystitis.

Dr. Richard Nguyen, MD
Abdominal Imaging
